scenes.text: In this introductory lecture on Docker, we will explore what Docker is and how it revolutionizes software deployment. Docker is a platform designed to create, deploy, and manage virtualized application containers on a common operating system (OS). This allows developers to package applications with all its dependencies and ship it as a single unit. We'll look at how Docker helps to avoid the 'it works on my machine' problem by ensuring consistent environments for development and production.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; D[Docker] --> C[Create] --> |package app w/ dependencies| E[Environment consistency]; D --> P[Deploy]; D --> M[Manage]
